query: Why do movies cost so much to make? Paying the actors aside how can it cost hundred of millions of dollars sometimes to make a movie?

It is no coincidence if VFXs can cost from a fifth up to a half of the overall budget, that is to say between 40 and over 100 million dollars. Even the costs for a single movie scene can range from 10.000 to well over 60.000 dollars. A VFX studioΓÇÖs exspenses Why are visual effects so expensive? Because they require so much labor and, above all, so much time. A large scale project may take over 200 people (all experienced and trained specialists, who are paid wealthy salaries) to work on  Answer:

baseline.final_answer: Movies can cost hundreds of millions of dollars to make due to various factors beyond actor salaries. Visual effects alone can account for a significant portion of the budget, ranging from 40 to over 100 million dollars, as they require extensive labor from highly skilled specialists and considerable time to produce. Additionally, the overall production process, including shooting around the world and making the film appear grand, contributes to the high costs. For instance, a Star Wars film can take three to four years to complete.

evaluation.feedback: The answer contains factual claims not supported by any citations. The answer should only contain information from the provided documents and cite them appropriately.
